purpose: External evidence that AI-assisted developer work is now a broad IDE/chat/CLI/cloud practice requiring explicit work domains.

claims: 84% of respondents are using or planning to use AI tools in development.
51% of professional developers report daily AI-tool use.
evidence.implication_for_ion: AI-assisted development is a mass work practice, not an edge case; ION needs a first-class assistant-work domain taxonomy.

claims: GitHub distinguishes synchronous IDE assistant work from cloud-agent work.
The docs name a tracking gap in local IDE assistant sessions unless decisions are committed.
evidence.implication_for_ion: ION should model local IDE sessions, cloud agents, commits/PRs, and lost-decision risk as separate state surfaces.

evidence.source_ref: VS Code Copilot agent mode blog
claims: Agent mode can analyze a codebase, read files, propose edits, run commands/tests, monitor output, and iterate on errors.
evidence.implication_for_ion: IDE-agent work spans codebase understanding, implementation, terminal/test loops, diagnostics, and validation domains.

claims: Cascade is described with Code/Chat modes, tool calling, checkpoints, awareness, and linter integration.
evidence.implication_for_ion: Modern IDE assistants have multiple modes and state surfaces; route and checkpoint protocols matter.

claims: Claude Code is described as an assistant/system that understands a codebase, works across files/tools, runs tests, and delivers committed code.
evidence.implication_for_ion: Assistant domains must include multi-file work, tool use, test proof, and commit/receipt boundaries.

claims: Codex is described as a coding agent that reads, edits, and runs code.
Codex cloud can work in background/parallel in its own environment; the IDE extension supports side-by-side IDE use and cloud delegation.
evidence.implication_for_ion: ION should distinguish side-by-side IDE, local CLI, cloud/background, and parallel execution embodiments.

claims: Agent skills package instructions, resources, and optional scripts for task-specific workflows.
evidence.implication_for_ion: ION should bind skill-like packages to domains, templates, protocols, and specialist agents rather than treating them as isolated prompts.

non_claims: This evidence map is not a full market survey.
The source list is a starting research context, not accepted ION canon.
External product claims are used only to shape the taxonomy, not to endorse any product.
